title: Guide Tones
subtitle: 3rds and 7ths through the A section
promise: Track only the 3rds and 7ths so the harmony starts making sense under your fingers.
why: Guide tones are the skeleton of jazz harmony. If you can hear and play these notes, the changes stop feeling like random chord names.

voicings.note: The minor sound is already here without the root.
This is the dominant engine. Hear the tension.
Resolution, but still elegant and light.
A soft lift into the next phrase.

practice: Play only the two guide tones for each chord.
Move slowly through cm7 -> F7 -> Bbmaj7 -> Ebmaj7.
End by playing lesson 001 shells once.

listening.note: Listen for the inner voice movement, not just the melody.
